articles.title: West Chicago teacher resigns following backlash over social media post supporting ICE
articles.author: Cindy Hernandez

key_people: Rita Balgeman (West Chicago Elementary School District 33 board president)
Kristina Davis (Superintendent, West Chicago Elementary School District 33)
Nestor Ruiz (interim chief of staff for Illinois State Senator Karina Villa)
Ana Galvan (parent of students at Gary Elementary School)
key_issues: Teacher conduct and social media use
School district response to controversial speech
Immigration enforcement debate (ICE) in the community
extraction.ai_summary: An elementary school teacher in West Chicago resigned after backlash over a social‑media post that appeared to support ICE. The district board accepted the resignation following administrative leave and public outcry, citing concerns about school climate and student safety. The case highlights tensions between free expression, immigration policy debates, and district governance in Chicago-area schools.

articles.title: Chicago avanza en la obtención de fondos para comprar la estación de Greyhound del centro
articles.author: David Struett

key_people: Bill Conway – Chicago City Councilmember (34th Ward)
Peter Strazzabosco – Vice Commissioner, Department of Planning and Development
key_issues: Funding and purchase of the Greyhound bus station
Use of Tax Increment Financing (TIF) to acquire the property
Municipal ownership and operation of an intercity bus terminal
Allocation of mayoral budget funds for the project
extraction.ai_summary: Chicago is moving to secure $50 million in TIF funding, plus mayoral allocations, to purchase and renovate the Greyhound station at 630 W. Harrison St. The plan, identified by Mayor Brandon Johnson and Councilmember Bill Conway, will be reviewed by the Community Development Commission and Finance Committee, aiming to give the city its first municipally‑run intercity bus terminal.
